role: Former Minister of Intelligence (1984–1989); head of Hajj and Pilgrimage Organisation
bio: Mohammad Reyshahri was Iran's first dedicated Minister of Intelligence, serving under Prime Minister [Mir-Hossein Mousavi](/people/mir-hossein-mousavi) and President [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) from 1984 to 1989. He established the Ministry of Intelligence (MOIS) as an institutional entity and presided over it during the 1988 mass executions of political prisoners — the bloodiest period of internal repression in the Islamic Republic's history. He later headed the Hajj and Pilgrimage Organisation. He ran for president in 1997 but received less than 0.5% of the vote. He is regarded as a founding architect of Iran's security state.
editorial_notes: Born 1946 in Rey. Established MOIS as minister 1984–1989. Oversaw ministry during 1988 political prisoner massacre. Ran in 1997 presidential election, received minimal votes.
